intro: Vanclevea stylosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Vanclevea stylosa are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Vanclevea stylosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Vanclevea stylosa is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and also used in the production of herbal infusions and tinctures.
